chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ حَجَّ عَنْ غَيْرِهِ إِنَّ لَهُ فِيهَا شِرْكَةً
chapter_title.en: Chapter 68 – The one who performs Hajj on behalf of someone else; there is a participation in it for him

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/226)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/226)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ الرَّجُلُ يَحُجُّ عَنْ آخَرَ مَا لَهُ مِنَ الْأَجْرِ وَ الثَّوَابِ قَالَ لِلَّذِي يَحُجُّ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَجْرُ وَ ثَوَابُ عَشْرِ حِجَجٍ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Hassan, from Ali Bin Yusuf, from Abu Abdullah Al momin, from Ibn Muskan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The man performs Hajj on behalf of another, what would be for him, from the Recompense and the Rewards?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘For the one who performs Hajj on behalf of another man would be Recompense and Rewards of ten Hajjs’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Hassan from Ali ibn Yusuf from abu ‘Abd Allah, al-Mu’min from ibn Muskan who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who performs al-Hajj for others; if he receives any reward. He (the Imam) said, ‘For the one who performs al-Hajj for another person, there, a reward for ten al-Hajj ah is for him.’”
